summary.intent: Fix a P2 bug in the FocusScope component where `Props::trapped` was silently ignored during activation, using TDD red-green workflow.
summary.outcome: The bug was fixed by making `Activate` read `props.trapped` alongside `event.trapped || props.contain`, all tests passed (including mutation testing with 0 missed mutants), and the change was committed and pushed.

repo: FocusScope's `Activate` handler reads `event.trapped || props.contain` but previously omitted `props.trapped`, creating a discrepancy between documented API and runtime behavior.
The project uses a Codex review system that produces prioritized findings (P1/P2) that feed back into the development loop.
Mutation testing is run as part of the pre-push gate alongside xci-fast; results are reported as caught/unviable/missed.

code.finding: `Activate` only checked `event.trapped || props.contain`, silently ignoring `props.trapped`. Fix adds `props.trapped` to the condition.
Spec was updated to align with the corrected behavior, indicating spec and implementation are kept in sync as part of the fix workflow.

workflow: TDD red-green cycle is enforced: failing test is written and confirmed red before applying the fix.
Mutation testing (23 caught, 11 unviable, 0 missed) is used to validate test suite quality after bug fixes, not just correctness.
Watcher/notification system for Codex findings can silently fail; foreground polling is a necessary fallback.

friction: The background watcher recorded a Codex verdict but failed to notify, requiring the user to prompt for foreground polling manually.
